blogPost.title: Samvera Stories: Franklin College announces the launch of the Thomasine Allen Online Collection

content.value: Digital repositories make local stories globally available.

At Franklin College, students partnered with library staff to launch the Thomasine Allen Online Collection using Hyku, gaining hands-on experience in digital scholarship while highlighting an important piece of institutional history.
As a PALNI-supported institution participating in Hyku for Consortia, Franklin College benefits from shared open-source infrastructure powered by the Samvera community.
Read more about the project and its impact
on the Hyku for Consortia website.

openGraphData.og:description: Samvera™ is a vibrant and welcoming community of information and technology professionals who share challenges, build expertise, and create sustainable, best-in-class solutions, making the world’s digital collections accessible now and into the future. Samvera’s suite of repository software tools offers flexible and rich user interfaces tailored to distinct content types on top of a robust back end – giving adopters the best of both worlds.
